summary: Threat model for system with 11 assets, 14 data flows, and 18 identified threats.

assets.name: React SPA
assets.description: Single page application serving the e-commerce storefront to end users

assets.name: CloudFront CDN
assets.description: Content delivery network caching static assets and routing requests

assets.name: API Gateway
assets.description: REST API gateway handling request routing, rate limiting, and API key validation

assets.name: Keycloak
assets.description: OpenID Connect identity provider managing user authentication and JWT issuance

assets.name: Order Service
assets.description: Node.js microservice handling order creation, updates, and fulfillment

assets.name: Payment Service
assets.description: Java microservice processing payments via Stripe, PCI DSS compliant

assets.name: Product Catalog Service
assets.description: Python FastAPI Lambda serving product data and search

assets.name: PostgreSQL (RDS)
assets.description: Relational database storing orders, users, and transaction data

assets.name: Redis Cache
assets.description: ElastiCache cluster for session management and product catalog caching

assets.name: S3 Bucket
assets.description: Object storage for product images and static assets

assets.name: SQS Queue
assets.description: Async message queue for order processing pipeline

data_flows.description: Static asset requests and API calls
Proxied API requests with origin authentication
OAuth2 authorization code flow for user login
Order CRUD operations with JWT validation

threats.name: SQL Injection in Order Service Database Queries
threats.description: If the Order Service constructs SQL queries using string concatenation with user-supplied input, an attacker could extract all customer data, modify orders, or drop tables.

mitigations.description: Use parameterized queries (prepared statements) for all database operations
Implement input validation with strict type checking and length limits
Deploy WAF rule to detect and block SQL injection patterns

threats.attack_vector: Attacker crafts malicious input in order fields that gets interpolated into SQL queries

threats.name: Credential Stuffing Against Keycloak Authentication
threats.description: Automated attacks using leaked credential databases could compromise customer accounts, enabling fraudulent orders and payment theft.

mitigations.description: Implement progressive rate limiting on failed login attempts per IP and per account
Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts
Integrate breached password detection service during registration and login

threats.attack_vector: Attacker uses leaked credential databases to attempt automated login

threats.name: JWT Token Manipulation for Privilege Escalation
threats.description: If JWT signatures aren't properly validated, an attacker could forge tokens granting admin access to order management and user data.

mitigations.description: Validate JWT signatures using RS256 with key rotation on every request
Implement claim validation (issuer, audience, expiration) in each microservice
Implement short-lived tokens (5-15 minutes) with refresh token rotation

threats.attack_vector: Attacker modifies JWT claims or exploits weak signature validation
